key_insight: TREK expands a student model’s reasoning support with verified proposals before reinforcement learning, helping GRPO escape hard prompts whose correct solutions are initially outside the on-policy support.
engineering_relevance: The method is practical because it can use an external teacher, a white-box teacher, or the model itself with extra context, making it easy to deploy when teacher internals are unavailable.
method: Find hard prompts where the student’s pass rate is very low.
Generate verified candidate solutions from a proposal source and keep the top-r proposals by student likelihood.
Apply a short forward-KL distillation phase to add those modes to support, then resume standard on-policy GRPO refinement.
evidence_signal: In the abstract, TREK improves Qwen3-8B on AIME 2025 from 36.9 to 40.3 and AIME 2024 from 47.9 to 51.1 (avg@16), and also boosts ALFWorld and ScienceWorld success rates.
watch_item: TREK depends on the quality of verified proposals and prompt filtering, so weak proposal generation or noisy verification could limit gains or add extra pipeline complexity.
paper_title: TREK: Distill to Explore, Reinforce to Refine
